fields.name: map name#Used to associate external resources with this map - e.g. PDA camera setting block names.
local sea level:wu#used to calculate aircraft altitude
sandbox origin point#forge coordinates are relative to this point
default vehicle set#when vehicle set is "map default," this vehicle set is used
Playtest req palette#requisition for SvE, activated via an init.txt option for playtest balance
SPAWN INFLUENCE OVERRIDES
fields.definition: You can use the following to override multiplayer global spawn influencers for the scenario.  See documentation of the various influencer effects (and default values) in multiplayer/multiplayer_globals.multiplayer_globals.

fields.name: global screen effect#ignores the falloff curves
atmosphere globals#settings that apply to the entire scenario
garbage collection
fields.definition: specify zero for values that should use the data in the globals tag.

fields.name: chud reference#for scenario specific chud elements
Size Class
fields.definition:
Tells lightmapper desired res for structure bitmaps.
Numbers in parens are final sizes after compression

fields.name: structure vertex sink#this is the most that we can sink a soft surface link snow in the structure_bsp via vertex painting.
default instance fade start pixels#>end, pixel size we start stippling out. code default 36
default instance fade end pixels#<start, pixel size where we no longer render. code default 30
Clones
fields.definition:
Describes which other bsps are physical 'clones' of this bsp
This is used to determine how to attach 'position-only' elements, like decorators, to the bsps:
Each clone gets a separate copy of decorators that are in both.
Non-cloned bsps cannot split decorators this way - the decorator will be given to the lowest numbered bsp

fields.name: custom gravity scale#0==nogravity, 1==full, set the custom gravity scale flag to make this parameter active
cloud scale#mapping to the world unit
Lightmap resolution for each buckets
fields.definition:
relative to world unit per texel. default:
1-> 1
2-> 4
2-> 16
3-> 64
5-> 128
6-> 256
7-> 512

fields.name: analytical light bounce modifier (when per bsp flag is on)
non analytical light bounce modifier (when per bsp flag is on)
extra lighting bsp flags#neighbor bsp that occlude or contribute light (including bounce light)
game portal to door occluder mapping
parent object#if an object with this name exists, we attach to it as a child
wave placement filter#Filter which squads in Firefight waves can be spawned into this squad
soon to be an initial task
CONTROLS~
fields.definition: Ctrl-N: Creates a new area and assigns it to the current selection of firing points.

fields.name: follow Z clamp:wus#Don't follow at areas outside of this vertical margin
maximum duration:seconds#Exhaust this task after it has been active for this long
exhaustion delay:seconds#When a task exhausts, hold actors in the task for this long before releasing them
Exhaustion Script#static script that is run when the task is exhausted
dialogue type#when someone enters this task for the first time, they play this type of dialogue
Kungfu count#The number of guys under this task that should be allowed to fight the player at a time
UI-Controlled
fields.definition: You don't need to modify these here. They are managed by the objectives ui

fields.name: lifetime count#task will never want to suck in more then n guys over lifetime (soft ceiling only applied when limit exceeded
max body count#task becomes inactive after the given number of casualties
min strength:[0,1]#task becomes inactive after the strength of the participants falls below the given level
initial movement distance#before doing anything else, the actor will travel the given distance in its forward direction
round range#the rounds for which we will consider this
set range#the sets for which we will consider this
delay:seconds#how long the AI should pause at this point
angle:degrees#the angle-from-forward that the AI can pick at this point
swarm count#number of cretures in swarm if a swarm is spawned at this location
initial movement distance#before doing anything else, the actor will travel the given distance in its forward direction
Multiplayer Data
fields.definition: object data for multiplayer game use

fields.name: Player Respawn Weight
fields.definition: This is valid only for objects which are used as player respawn locations

fields.name: Character Build
fields.definition: Specify the distribution of characters and weapons in the cell sub-folders

fields.name: normal diff count#initial number of actors on normal difficulty

health station charges#if this control is a health station, this sets the number of charges it contains.
Use 0 for infinite
attenuation distances
fields.definition: these settings vary how the sound fades as you move closer or further away from it.

fields.name: don't obstruct distance:world units#don't obstruct below this distance
don't play distance:world units#don't play below this distance
attack distance:world units#start playing at full volume at this distance
minimum distance:world units#start attenuating at this distance
sustain begin distance:world units#set attenuation to sustain db at this distance
sustain end distance:world units#continue attenuating to silence at this distance
maximum distance:world units#the distance beyond which this sound is no longer audible
sustain db:dB#the amount of attenuation between sustain begin and end
~controls
fields.definition: some descriptive text will go here... eventually

fields.name: minimum thorage version#if greater than 0, this won't be shown in regular builds unless the Thorage version is at least equal to
minimum thorage version#if greater than 0, this won't be shown in regular builds unless the Thorage version is at least equal to
Submenu#controls which requisition submenu this object should appear in
maximum allowed#cant buy more if there are too many in play
built-in palettes for which item is enabled by default
naming
fields.definition:
the name of the trigger volume specifies what kind of volume it is:
'zone_set:'
'begin_zone_set:'
'kill' (plus '_soft' for soft kill volume)
'safe_zone' (plus '_soft' for soft kill volume)
'location_'

fields.name: z sink#this is only valid for sector type trigger volumes
weather sound#plays when rain is active, weather rate gets applied to scale.
entry sound#plays when entering this area
exit sound#plays when leaving this area
override falloff function#Multiplier applied to weight (domain is full weight radius to fall-off radius, range should be 0 to 1).
hold time{occupy time}:seconds#How long the AI should pause at this point before searching
search time:seconds#How long the AI should search at this point before returning
pause time:seconds#How long the AI should pause at this point after searching before moving on
cooldown time:seconds#How long after being abandoned should this point be available again
Distribution
fields.definition: The following blocks describe who will receive this cue.

fields.name: Payload
fields.definition: The following blocks describe the type of stimulus and related payload; you should only specify one.

fields.name: dialogue type#when this ending is triggered, launch a dialogue event of the given type
dialogue type#when this ending is triggered, launch a dialogue event of the given type
cluster camera_fx tag:if empty, uses default#if empty, uses default
forced exposure:stops#the target exposure (ONLY USED WHEN FORCE EXPOSURE IS CHECKED)
forced auto-exposure screen brightness:[0.0001-1]#how bright you want the screen to be (ONLY USED WHEN FORCE AUTO EXPOSURE IS CHECKED)
ecology margin:wus#distance from ecology boundary that creature begins to be repulsed
production frequency bounds:boids/sec#How frequently boids are produced at one of the sources (limited by the max boid count)
source scale to 0 radius:wus#Distance from a source at which the creature scales to full size
sink scale to 0 radius:wus#Distance from a sink at which the creature begins to scale to zero
flock destroy duration:sec#The number of seconds it takes to kill all units in the flock if it gets destroyed
weight#probability of producing at this source
max destination volume penetration:wu#The farthest the boid will go inside our destination volume
move distance#drop height for drop to ground
angle snap interval#rotate brushes will snap to intervals of this
KEYS
fields.definition:
[] 	 brush size
-= 	 brush feathering
0-9 	 brush opacity
SHIFT+0-9	 select palette
SHIFT	 while painting will reverse the operation

fields.name: run acceleration:world units per second squared
sneak acceleration:world units per second squared
airborne acceleration:world units per second squared
grenade aiming#determines the distance along the aiming vector to orient the grenade based on the camera pitch
fire team cone angle: in degrees
seconds to start#how long you must be pegged before you start sprinting
seconds to full speed#how long you must sprint before you reach top speed
decay rate#how fast being unpegged decays the timer (seconds per second)
full speed multiplier#how much faster we actually go when at full sprint
sprint turn multiplier#how much faster to turn when sprinting
sprint max look yaw velocity:degress per second
sprint max look pitch velocity:degress per second
minimum player velocity to be considered in a sprinting state:world units per second
sprint window length:seconds#period of time over which we record the biped's look angle for deciding if we should drop him out of sprint
time#if you finish in under this time you get the following bonus
script name#The name of a custom script used to drive the performance. If none is given, a default script is uses that goes through the lines in sequence
global name#The name of a script global that will be declared for this performance.
thespian radius{performance radius}#The radius inside which actors have to be in order to start.
attraction radius#The radius inside which actors become attracted to the thespian origin.
attraction probability per second:[0,1]#The probability that an actor be attracted once inside the thespian radius, every second.
fragment#maximum 256 characters, type just branch condition (with brackets) in case of branches
branch target#the script to branch to (with any arguments to it). Used only if type is branch
transition frame count#The number of frames from the end of the animation to start transitioning out
Garbage Times
fields.definition: time before the game considers that following entities as garbage

options: always draw sky#always draw sky 0, even if no +sky polygons are visible
don't strip pathfinding#always leave pathfinding in, even for a multiplayer scenario
big vehicle use center point for light sampling
in space#so we can hide hud elements like the compass
survival#so we can strip the elite from the global player representations
do not strip variants#so we can test the impact of variant stripping
ray trace adjacent bsps on sky hits
lightmaps final gather ignores backfacing hits
not a normally playable space in an MP map (check this on shared BSPs)
dont use extra lighting bsps for cubemaps
analytical bounce uses per bsp setting
hidden#this palette is only visible in superforge, and objects within it are only editable in superforge
use adjacent cluster as portal scale
use adjacent cluster as exterior scale
If ALL players in trigger volume
Player shot more than X seconds ago
